items.prompt_md: A list you repeatedly `.append()` to occasionally has to resize (copy every existing element into a bigger backing array). Why is n appends still O(n) total, not O(n²)?
options: Because resizes happen less and less often as the array grows, so the copying cost spread over all n appends averages to O(1) each
Because every individual `.append()` call is O(n) on its own
Because the list is sorted before each append
items.answer: Because resizes happen less and less often as the array grows, so the copying cost spread over all n appends averages to O(1) each
items.explanation_md: Resizes get exponentially rarer as the backing array grows (doubling means only ~log n resizes across n appends). Summing the occasional O(n) resize cost across all n appends and dividing by n still gives O(1) per append on average — that's the amortized cost, even though a handful of individual calls are more expensive.

items.prompt_md: You're deciding how much *extra* memory an approach uses beyond the input array, in a case where you only need to remember the best value seen so far. Which approach fits?
options: Track a couple of running variables, like a min-so-far and a best-so-far — O(1) extra space
Build and store a full second array of running results the same length as the input — O(n) extra space
Sort a copy of the input first — O(n) extra space and O(n log n) time
Recompute the answer from scratch at every step by rescanning everything before it
items.answer: Track a couple of running variables, like a min-so-far and a best-so-far — O(1) extra space
items.explanation_md: "Only need what I've seen so far" is the signal that a fixed handful of running variables suffices — no auxiliary array that grows with n, so the extra space stays O(1) no matter how large the input gets.

items.prompt_md: What are the time and space complexity of the one-pass "track the minimum price seen so far" solution to Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock?
items.explanation_md: One pass over the prices is O(n) time. The only memory used is two running scalars (`min_price` and `best`), which doesn't grow with the input size, so space is O(1).
